question_text: A steel bar 35 mm x 35 mm in section and 100 mm in length is acted upon by a tensile load of 180 kN along its longitudinal axis and 400 kN and 300 kN along the axis of the lateral surfaces. Determine : (i) change in the dimensions of the bar, (ii) change in volume, (iii) longitudinal axial load acting alone to produce the same longitudinal strain as in (i). Take E = 205 GPa; ν = 0.3.
question_latex: A steel bar $35\text{ mm} \times 35\text{ mm}$ in section and $100\text{ mm}$ in length is acted upon by a tensile load of $180\text{ kN}$ along its longitudinal axis and $400\text{ kN}$ and $300\text{ kN}$ along the axis of the lateral surfaces. Determine : (i) change in the dimensions of the bar, (ii) change in volume, (iii) longitudinal axial load acting alone to produce the same longitudinal strain as in (i). Take $E = 205\text{ GPa}$; $\nu = 0.3$.
subject: Basics of Mechanical Engineering

question_text: A furnace wall consists of 120 mm thick refractory brick and 120 mm thick insulating firebrick separated by an air gap. The outside wall is covered with a 10 mm thickness of plaster. The inner surface of the wall is at 1000 C and the room temperature is 20 C. Calculate the rate at which heat is lost per m2 of the wall surface. The heat transfer coefficient from the outside wall surface to the air in the room is 20 W/m2K, and the resistance to heat flow of the air gap is 0.15 K/W. The thermal conductivity of refractory brick, insulating firebrick, and plaster are 1.6, 0.3, and 0.14 W/mK, respectively. Also, calculate each interface temperature of the outside of the wall.
question_latex: A furnace wall consists of $120\text{ mm}$ thick refractory brick and $120\text{ mm}$ thick insulating firebrick separated by an air gap. The outside wall is covered with a $10\text{ mm}$ thickness of plaster. The inner surface of the wall is at $1000^{\circ}\text{C}$ and the room temperature is $20^{\circ}\text{C}$. Calculate the rate at which heat is lost per $\text{m}^2$ of the wall surface. The heat transfer coefficient from the outside wall surface to the air in the room is $20\text{ W/m}^2\text{K}$, and the resistance to heat flow of the air gap is $0.15\text{ K/W}$. The thermal conductivity of refractory brick, insulating firebrick, and plaster are $1.6$, $0.3$, and $0.14\text{ W/mK}$, respectively. Also, calculate each interface temperature of the outside of the wall.
subject: Basics of Mechanical Engineering
